body:
Title: CRA Form T2203 2025 Saskatchewan, Alberta, and British Columbia multiple-jurisdiction tax.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/t2203/t2203-25e.pdf

CRA T2203 2025 Form SK428MJ calculates Saskatchewan tax for multiple jurisdictions. Normalized numeric values used by the encoded formulas include 0.105, 0.125, 0.145, and 0.50. SK428MJ tax brackets use thresholds $53,463 and $152,750; rates 10.5%, 12.5%, and 14.5%; and base amounts $0.00, $5,613.62, and $18,024.49. Lines 10 to 18 subtract the farm and small business capital gains tax credit, add unallocated tax on split income, and subtract non-refundable credits, dividend tax credit, and minimum tax carryover at 50%. Lines 19 to 22 add additional tax for minimum tax purposes at 50% and multiply by the Saskatchewan allocation percentage. Lines 23 to 32 apply the resident adjustment based on line 58360 at 10.5%, dividend tax credit, the percentage not allocated to Saskatchewan, and provincial foreign tax credit. Lines 35 to 46 subtract the political contribution tax credit, labour-sponsored venture capital tax credit capped at $875, mineral exploration tax credit, graduate tuition tax credit, and qualifying environmental trust tax credit, then enter Saskatchewan tax on Part 5 line 7.

CRA T2203 2025 Form AB428MJ calculates Alberta tax for multiple jurisdictions. Normalized numeric values used by the encoded formulas include 0.08, 0.10, 0.12, 0.13, 0.14, 0.15, and 0.35. AB428MJ tax brackets use thresholds $60,000, $151,234, $181,481, $241,974, and $362,961; rates 8%, 10%, 12%, 13%, 14%, and 15%; and base amounts including $4,800.00, $13,923.40, and $17,553.04. Lines 17 to 25 add unallocated tax on split income, subtract non-refundable credits, dividend tax credit, and minimum tax carryover at 35%, and add additional tax for minimum tax purposes at 35%. Lines 26 to 38 multiply by the Alberta allocation percentage, apply the resident adjustment based on lines 58330 and 58360 at 8%, dividend tax credit, the percentage not allocated to Alberta, and provincial foreign tax credit. Lines 40 to 43 subtract Alberta political contributions tax credit and supplemental tax credit, then enter Alberta tax on Part 5 line 8.

CRA T2203 2025 Form BC428MJ calculates British Columbia tax for multiple jurisdictions. Normalized numeric values used by the encoded formulas include 0.0506, 0.077, 0.105, 0.1229, 0.147, 0.168, 0.205, 0.349, and 0.0356. BC428MJ tax brackets use thresholds $49,279, $98,560, $113,158, $137,407, $186,306, and $259,829; rates 5.06%, 7.7%, 10.5%, 12.29%, 14.7%, 16.8%, and 20.5%; and base amounts $0.00, $2,493.52, $6,288.15, $7,820.94, $10,801.15, $17,989.30, and $30,341.16. Lines 17 to 25 add unallocated tax on split income, subtract non-refundable credits, dividend tax credit, and minimum tax carryover at 34.9%, and add additional tax for minimum tax purposes at 34.9%. Lines 26 to 38 multiply by the British Columbia allocation percentage, apply the resident adjustment based on lines 58315, 58316, 58330, and 58360 at 5.06%, dividend tax credit, the percentage not allocated to British Columbia, and provincial foreign tax credit. Lines 39 to 47 calculate the British Columbia tax reduction using a $562 basic reduction, $25,020 base amount, 3.56% rate, and the British Columbia allocation percentage. Lines 49 to 59 subtract logging tax credit, political contribution tax credit, ESOP and EVCC credits capped at $2,000, and mining flow-through share tax credit, then enter British Columbia tax on Part 5 line 9.
